topic: Two coworkers discover they went to the same summer camp
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office seemed to hum at an unnatural pitch. Sarah stared at the shared calendar, her finger tracing a date marked "Summer Camp Adventure." Beside it, Mark’s name was scrawled in a hasty, almost frantic handwriting. A cold knot tightened in her stomach as she reread the entry. It was impossible. She had never attended any summer camp, not even the regional ones. She pushed her chair back, the scrape of metal on linoleum echoing too loudly in the quiet breakroom. Her breath hitched. She looked up, meeting Mark’s gaze across the room. His eyes, usually so easygoing, were wide, fixed on her with an intensity that made her want to retreat. "Mark," she managed, her voice sounding thin and reedy. "What is this?"

[story 2]

I nearly dropped my coffee mug. The grainy photo on my screen showed a muddy field, brightly colored tents, and a group of kids laughing—and there, unmistakably, was my face. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. I frantically scrolled through my memories, searching for any recollection of such an experience. Nothing. This was a complete fabrication, a bizarre prank. Yet, the feeling washing over me wasn't amusement; it was a sudden, sharp sense of disorientation, like the ground beneath my feet had shifted. I leaned closer to my monitor, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the desk. "You were there?" I asked, the words coming out sharper than intended, laced with a tremor I couldn't quite suppress.

[story 3]

The email notification popped up, stark white against the dark background of my desktop. The subject line was simply: "Camp Reunion Photos." My hands started to shake so badly I had to press them flat against the keyboard to steady them. Every nerve ending felt hyper-aware, buzzing with an unwelcome energy. I clicked the attachment. The image displayed was undeniably me, standing next to a group of unfamiliar faces under a canopy of stars. A wave of profound unease washed over me, chilling me to the bone despite the warmth of the office. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my cubicle, my gaze darting around the familiar, mundane surroundings as if expecting the camp counselors to materialize from behind the filing cabinets.
